content: Debby: Hey there, stranger. Can I interest you in some of our fine cuisine? We've got everything from radroach steaks to super mutant stew. Harborman: Radroach steaks? Sounds like a delicacy. But I'll have to pass. I'm just here to relax and enjoy the atmosphere. Debby: Suit yourself. But you know what they say, in a post-apocalyptic world, you've got to make do with what you've got. Harborman: I understand. It's a tough world out there. Debby: You know, I've been working here for years. I've seen it all. But I still can't believe how different things are now. Porn and prostitution everywhere, women dressing like sluts... It's wild. Harborman: You don't seem too upset about it. Debby: Oh, I'm not.

Harborman: I see, so life in Far Harbor isn't so bad after all. There are worse places to be during these trying times. Debby: Yeah, it could be a lot worse. At least we've still got each other, and people passing through to keep us company. You know, the Captain came in here the other day, left a hat behind. Probably forgetful old codger. Harborman: You might want to keep that one, sounds like a good luck charm to me. Debby: Could be. Thanks for stopping by, stranger. Maybe I'll see you again sometime. Harborman: You're welcome. You bet, I'll be around. Have a good night. Debby: Y'know what, maybe I shouldn't have said that about radroach steaks. Those could actually be pretty good.  But for now, I better go check on Tink.

Debby: You know, I can't believe how different things are now. It's like the whole world has turned upside down. Ivana: That's true. But I guess we have to adapt to survive, right? Debby: Yeah, I guess so. But sometimes I miss the way things used to be. You know, when people weren't always looking for their next fix or their next conquest. Ivana: I understand. But in this world, survival is everything. And sometimes, that means doing things we never thought we'd have to do. Debby: I guess you're right. It's just... it's hard to keep going when everything around you is so messed up. Ivana: I know it is. But we're strong, Debby. We'll make it through this. Debby: Thanks, Ivana.

Debby: Yeah, you're right. We've been through worse, haven't we? Ivana: We sure have. And we'll keep on going, no matter what comes our way. Debby: That's the spirit. Let's just focus on making the most of what we have here and now. Ivana: Absolutely. Because in this world, that's all we can do. Debby: At least we still have radroach steaks, right? Ivana: Oh, Debby. You always know how to lighten the mood. Let's just enjoy our night and see what tomorrow brings. Debby: Sounds good to me. Cheers, Ivana. 

As the conversation fades, Debby goes to check on her cat, Tink, and Ivana resumes her duties as a server at the Last Plank. MJR, the protagonist, overhears the conversation and ponders on the resilience of the characters in this post-apocalyptic world.
